# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership

## System Context

Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned.

### Capabilities

| Capability | Owner | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool |
| done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages |
| completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected |
| ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` |
| ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` |

### Setup-Dependent Behavior

| Setup | in_progress | done | completed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual |
| Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) |

## C4 System Context (L1)

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer", "Commits code locally")
    Person(lead, "Team Lead", "Reviews PRs, monitors task board")

    System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph with task lifecycle management")

    System_Ext(github, "GitHub", "Remote git hosting, webhooks")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent", "AI coding agent with MCP tools")

    Rel(dev, brain, "Commits trigger status updates via", "post-commit hook")
    Rel(claude, brain, "Sets in_progress via", "MCP update_task_status")
    Rel(github, brain, "Pushes trigger done/completed via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(dev, github, "Pushes commits to", "git push")
    Rel(lead, brain, "Views task status via", "Web UI")
```

## C4 Container (L2)

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Container: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent")

    System_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(cli, "Brain CLI", "Bun binary", "commit-check command, git hooks, MCP server")
        Container(server, "Brain Server", "Bun HTTP", "API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator")
        ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "SurrealDB v3", "Tasks, sessions, git_commits, relations")
    }

    Rel(claude, cli, "Calls tools via", "MCP stdio")
    Rel(cli, server, "Calls API via", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(dev, cli, "Triggers via", "git post-commit hook")
    Rel(github, server, "Sends push events via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(server, surreal, "Reads/writes via", "SurrealDB SDK")
    Rel(cli, surreal, "No direct access", "")
```

## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Component: Task Status Transition Ownership

    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Agent")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    Person(dev, "Developer")

    Container_Boundary(cli_boundary, "Brain CLI") {
        Component(commit_check, "commit-check", "CLI command", "Parses task refs from commit, calls API to set done")
        Component(post_commit_hook, "post-commit hook", "Shell script", "Invokes brain commit-check fire-and-forget")
        Component(mcp_server, "MCP Server", "stdio", "Exposes update_task_status tool")
    }

    Container_Boundary(server_boundary, "Brain Server") {
        Component(mcp_route, "MCP Route", "HTTP handler", "Receives task status updates from CLI")
        Component(session_lifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Module", "Manages abort/reject backward transitions")
        Component(create_session, "createAgentSession", "Function", "Links task to session WITHOUT status change")
        Component(webhook_handler, "GitHub Webhook Handler", "HTTP handler", "Receives push events")
        Component(commit_processor, "Commit Processor", "Module", "Parses task refs, sets done or completed")
        Component(task_ref_parser, "commit-task-refs", "Pure function", "Regex extraction of task:id from messages")
    }

    Rel(claude, mcp_server, "Calls update_task_status", "MCP")
    Rel(mcp_server, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(dev, post_commit_hook, "Triggers on commit", "git hook")
    Rel(post_commit_hook, commit_check, "Invokes", "shell exec")
    Rel(commit_check, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(github, webhook_handler, "Sends push event", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(webhook_handler, commit_processor, "Delegates to", "async")
    Rel(commit_processor, task_ref_parser, "Extracts task IDs from", "function call")
    Rel(session_lifecycle, create_session, "No longer sets in_progress", "removed")
```

## Component Architecture

### Changes by Module

#### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update
- **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record
- **Keep**: Workspace scope validation
- **Traces to**: US-1, R1

#### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task
- **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session
- **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup
- **Traces to**: US-2, R1

#### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck()
- **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`)
- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
- **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error
- **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`
- **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts`
- **Traces to**: US-3, R3

#### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation
- **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook
- **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check)
- **Traces to**: US-4, R4

#### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing
- **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias)
- **Traces to**: US-3, US-4

#### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit()
- **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch:
  - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done`
  - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed`
- **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch`
- **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6

#### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook()
- **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`)
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

#### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult
- **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts

The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun.

**No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`).

### Idempotent Status Transitions

Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers.

### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution)

| Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done |
| Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done |
| Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed |
| Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed |

## Technology Stack

No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack:

| Component | Technology | License | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime |
| Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime |
| Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store |
| Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists |
| HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists |

## Integration Patterns

### CLI to Server (commit-check)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status`
- **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow)
- **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }`
- **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0.

### GitHub to Server (webhook)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing)
- **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler)
- **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}`
- **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done`

### Agent to Server (in_progress)
- **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing)
- **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes)
- **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes)

## Quality Attribute Strategies

### Reliability
- **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor
- **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow
- **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged

### Maintainability
- **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source
- **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication
- **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists

### Observability
- **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA
- **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking

### Testability
- **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation
- **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient`
- **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions

## Deployment Architecture

No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

DESIGN wave delivered. Here's a summary:

**Architecture** (`docs/design/task-status-ownership/architecture.md`):
- C4 diagrams (L1 context, L2 container, L3 component) in Mermaid
- 8 modules with exact change descriptions per module
- Idempotent forward-only status ordering resolves commit-check/webhook race conditions
- No new technology — all existing stack reused

**ADR** (`docs/adrs/ADR-009-task-status-ownership.md`):
- 3 alternatives evaluated and rejected with rationale

**Roadmap** (`docs/design/task-status-ownership/roadmap.yaml`):
- 3 phases, 6 steps, 7 production files (step ratio 0.86)
- Phase 1: Remove server-side forward transitions (US-1, US-2)
- Phase 2: CLI commit-check + post-commit hook (US-3, US-4)
- Phase 3: GitHub processor done/completed by branch (US-5, US-6)

Ready to commit and proceed to the next wave?

content.text:
Execute *design-architecture for task-status-ownership.

## Context

This is a backend refactoring of task status lifecycle ownership in an existing system. The project is a TypeScript/Bun application with SurrealDB, using a functional programming paradigm (already set in CLAUDE.md).

### Requirements Summary
Forward task transitions (in_progress, done, completed) move from server-owned to agent/processor-owned. Backward transitions (abort, reject → ready) stay server-owned.

| Transition | Owner | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| → in_progress | Agent | brain-start-task command |
| → done | commit-check hook (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | git commit with task refs |
| → completed | GitHub processor | merge to main |
| → ready (abort) | Server | Session aborted |
| → ready (reject) | Server | Session rejected |

### User Stories (6 total)
- US-1: Remove server-side in_progress from createAgentSession()
- US-2: Remove server-side done from acceptOrchestratorSession()  
- US-3: Add brain commit-check CLI command (two-pass: regex fast path, then LLM fallback)
- US-4: Wire commit-check as git post-commit hook
- US-5: GitHub processor sets done on push to non-main branches
- US-6: GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main

### Key Constraints
- commit-check must not block git (fire-and-forget, exit 0 on errors)
- Idempotent transitions (both commit-check and GitHub processor may fire for same commit)
- Functional programming paradigm
- Existing codebase uses Bun, TypeScript, SurrealDB, Vercel AI SDK

### Existing Code Locations
- `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` - createAgentSession() with in_progress transition (lines 546-561)
- `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` - acceptOrchestratorSession() with done transition (lines 557-562), abortOrchestratorSession() and rejectOrchestratorSession() with backward transitions
- `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` - processCommit() creates implemented_by relations
- `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` - extractReferencedTaskIds() regex parser
- `cli/commands/init-content.ts` - brain-start-task slash command
- `cli/mcp-server.ts` - update_task_status MCP tool
- `cli/http-client.ts` - Brain HTTP client

### Requirements files
- docs/requirements/task-status-ownership/requirements.md
- docs/requirements/task-status-ownership/user-stories.md
- docs/requirements/task-status-ownership/acceptance-criteria.md
- docs/ux/task-status-ownership/jtbd-job-stories.md
- docs/ux/task-status-ownership/jtbd-four-forces.md
- docs/ux/task-status-ownership/jtbd-opportunity-scores.md

## Instructions

1. Read the requirements files and existing code to understand the current architecture
2. This is NOT greenfield - analyze the existing codebase first
3. Skip paradigm selection (already functional)
4. Skip Steps 1-3.5 (we've already discussed drivers/constraints in the DISCUSS wave)
5. Focus on Step 4 (architecture recommendation) and Step 6 (deliverables)
6. Produce:
   - Architecture design document with C4 diagrams (Mermaid)
   - Component boundaries showing what changes in each existing module
   - ADR for the ownership model decision
7. Write outputs to docs/design/task-status-ownership/
8. Do NOT be interactive - we've already made all decisions in the DISCUSS wave. Just produce the deliverables.

1→---
     2→name: architecture-patterns
     3→description: Comprehensive architecture patterns, methodologies, quality frameworks, and evaluation methods for solution architects. Load when designing system architecture or selecting patterns.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Architecture Patterns and Methodologies
     7→
     8→## C4 Model -- Hierarchical Architecture Visualization
     9→
    10→Four levels for different audiences:
    11→1. **System Context**: system + users + external systems (stakeholder view)
    12→2. **Containers**: applications, data stores, deployment units (technical overview)
    13→3. **Components**: internal modules within containers (developer view)
    14→4. **Code**: class/module level (optional, often auto-generated)
    15→
    16→Notation/tooling independent. Reduces communication overhead, shared visual language across stakeholders.
    17→
    18→## Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)
    19→
    20→Isolate business logic from infrastructure through ports (interfaces) and adapters (implementations).
    21→
    22→- **Ports**: technology-agnostic interfaces for external communication
    23→- **Primary ports** (driving): REST controllers, CLI handlers, message consumers -- inbound
    24→- **Secondary ports** (driven): DB repos, external service clients, filesystem -- outbound
    25→- **Adapters**: technology-specific port implementations
    26→
    27→Benefits: testability (isolated core) | flexibility (swap infrastructure) | technology independence | maintainability
    28→
    29→Testing: unit tests through driving ports, mock driven ports | integration tests with real infrastructure | acceptance tests end-to-end through primary ports
    30→
    31→## Architectural Pattern Selection
    32→
    33→### Layered Architecture
    34→Horizontal layers with defined dependencies. Use for: traditional enterprise apps, clear separation. Trade-off: familiar but potential overhead, layer coupling.
    35→
    36→### Microservices
    37→Independent deployable services per capability. Use for: large teams, component scaling, tech diversity. Trade-off: scalability vs operational complexity. 2025 consensus: "start monolith, evolve when needed." Modular monolith = valid middle ground.
    38→
    39→### Event-Driven Architecture
    40→Components communicate via events through broker. Use for: real-time, complex processes, loose coupling. Trade-off: scalability/decoupling vs event ordering, debugging.
    41→
    42→### CQRS + Event Sourcing
    43→Separate read/write models; store events not state. Use for: financial, audit, temporal queries. Trade-off: complete history + independent scaling vs eventual consistency + complexity. NOT for: simple CRUD, strong consistency, inexperienced teams.
    44→
    45→## Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
    46→
    47→### Strategic Patterns
    48→- **Bounded Context**: explicit boundaries for domain model; prevents "single unified model" trap
    49→- **Context Mapping**: Shared Kernel | Customer/Supplier | Anti-Corruption Layer | Open Host Service
    50→
    51→### Tactical Patterns
    52→- **Aggregates**: consistency/transactional boundaries; root = only entry point
    53→- **Domain Events**: represent occurrences; enable loose coupling between contexts
    54→
    55→### Identifying Boundaries
    56→Language differences between departments | representation differences | consistency requirements define aggregate boundaries. Bounded contexts often map to microservice boundaries and team ownership.
    57→
    58→## ISO 25010 Quality Attributes
    59→
    60→Eight characteristics:
    61→1. **Functional Suitability**: completeness, correctness, appropriateness
    62→2. **Performance Efficiency**: time behavior, resource utilization, capacity
    63→3. **Compatibility**: coexistence, interoperability
    64→4. **Usability**: learnability, operability, accessibility
    65→5. **Reliability**: maturity, availability, fault tolerance, recoverability
    66→6. **Security**: confidentiality, integrity, non-repudiation, accountability, authenticity
    67→7. **Maintainability**: modularity, reusability, analyzability, modifiability, testability
    68→8. **Portability**: adaptability, installability, replaceability
    69→
    70→Trade-offs: Security vs Performance | Scalability vs Consistency (CAP) | Flexibility vs Performance | Usability vs Security
    71→
    72→Application: identify priority attributes, define measurable requirements, analyze trade-offs, validate with ATAM.
    73→
    74→## ATAM (Architecture Trade-off Analysis Method)
    75→
    76→Systematic evaluation from SEI/CMU.
    77→
    78→**Phase 1 - Presentation**: business drivers, architecture approaches, design decisions
    79→**Phase 2 - Investigation**: quality attribute scenarios, evaluate approaches, identify sensitivity/trade-off points
    80→**Phase 3 - Testing**: prioritize scenarios, analyze top in depth, document risks/non-risks
    81→
    82→Key concepts: **Sensitivity Point** (impacts one attribute) | **Trade-off Point** (affects multiple attributes) | **Architectural Risk** (may prevent attribute achievement)
    83→
    84→CBAM extends ATAM with economic analysis (ROI-driven). Perform early when cost of change is minimal. Lightweight: Mini-ATAM (half-day workshop).
    85→
    86→## Cloud Resilience Patterns
    87→
    88→### Circuit Breaker
    89→Monitor failures; after threshold fail fast ("open"); periodically test recovery. States: Closed, Open, Half-Open. Prevents cascading failures.
    90→
    91→### Retry with Exponential Backoff
    92→1s, 2s, 4s, 8s + jitter. Only transient errors, not business logic. Operations must be idempotent.
    93→
    94→### Bulkhead
    95→Isolate elements into pools; one failure doesn't affect others. Separate connection/thread pools per feature/tenant.
    96→
    97→### Throttling
    98→Rate limiting, concurrency limiting, resource quotas per user/tenant/service.
    99→
   100→### Saga Pattern
   101→Distributed transactions as local transaction sequence with compensating rollbacks. Choreography (decentralized) vs Orchestration (centralized).
   102→
   103→## API Architecture: REST vs GraphQL
   104→
   105→**REST**: resource-based URLs, HTTP verbs, stateless, standard caching. Best for: public APIs, simple CRUD, caching-critical.
   106→**GraphQL**: single endpoint, client-specified queries, typed schema. Best for: mobile (bandwidth), nested data, rapid frontend iteration.
   107→**Hybrid**: GraphQL gateway aggregating REST/RPC backends.
   108→Security for GraphQL: query depth limiting, complexity analysis, timeout, field-level auth.
   109→
   110→## ADR Templates
   111→
   112→**Nygard** (most common): Title, Status (Proposed/Accepted/Deprecated/Superseded), Context, Decision, Consequences
   113→**MADR** (extended): adds trade-off analysis, considered options with pros/cons
   114→**Y-Statement** (concise): "In context of [use case], facing [concern], decided for [option] to achieve [quality], accepting [downside]"
   115→
   116→Best practices: single decision per ADR | immutable (supersede, never modify) | store in VCS | create when decided
   117→
   118→## Technology Selection: Open Source Priority
   119→
   120→Evaluation order: 1. Mature OSS with strong community | 2. Newer OSS with active dev | 3. Proprietary only when specified or no viable OSS
   121→
   122→OSS criteria: last commit <6 months | regular releases | quick issue resolution | 10+ regular contributors | >1000 GitHub stars for critical components
   123→
   124→License preference: MIT > Apache 2.0 > BSD > MPL 2.0 > LGPL (caution) > GPL (careful) > AGPL (extreme caution). Proprietary forbidden without explicit request.
   125→
   126→Document per selection: name/version, license, GitHub URL/stats, maintenance assessment, alternatives considered.
   127→

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→

1→# Requirements: Task Status Ownership
     2→
     3→## Problem Statement
     4→
     5→Task status transitions are duplicated between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors. The server optimistically sets in_progress on assignment and done on session accept, but these are redundant with the agent's brain-start-task command and the GitHub commit processor. This creates confusion about who owns the truth and causes orphaned states on agent crashes.
     6→
     7→## Design Principle
     8→
     9→**Forward transitions are agent/processor-owned. Backward transitions are server-owned.**
    10→
    11→The entity doing the work owns progress signals. The orchestrator owns recovery.
    12→
    13→| Transition | Owner | Trigger |
    14→|---|---|---|
    15→| → in_progress | Agent | brain-start-task command |
    16→| → done | commit-check hook (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | git commit with task refs |
    17→| → completed | GitHub processor | merge to main |
    18→| → ready (abort) | Server | Session aborted |
    19→| → ready (reject) | Server | Session rejected |
    20→
    21→## Requirements
    22→
    23→### R1: Remove server-side forward transitions
    24→
    25→Remove the in_progress transition from `createAgentSession()` in `mcp-queries.ts`. The server should still link `source_session` to the task but not change status.
    26→
    27→Remove the done transition from `acceptOrchestratorSession()` in `session-lifecycle.ts`. Session accept is a review gate, not a completion event.
    28→
    29→### R2: Agent owns in_progress
    30→
    31→`brain-start-task` already instructs the agent to call `update_task_status` → in_progress. This becomes the sole authority. No changes needed here.
    32→
    33→### R3: Add `brain commit-check` CLI command
    34→
    35→New CLI command that:
    36→1. Reads the most recent commit message (or accepts a commit SHA)
    37→2. **Fast path**: parses task refs using existing `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `commit-task-refs.ts`
    38→3. **LLM fallback**: if no explicit refs found, uses the same LLM analysis as the GitHub commit processor to infer which task(s) the commit relates to
    39→4. Calls the Brain API to set each referenced/inferred task's status → done
    40→5. Idempotent — setting done on an already-done task is a no-op
    41→
    42→### R4: Wire commit-check as git post-commit hook
    43→
    44→Install `brain commit-check` as a git post-commit hook, similar to how Brain already manages pre-commit hooks. The hook calls the Brain HTTP API.
    45→
    46→### R5: GitHub commit processor sets done on push
    47→
    48→Extend `github-commit-processor.ts` to set task status → done (in addition to creating `implemented_by` relations) when commits are pushed to non-main branches.
    49→
    50→### R6: GitHub commit processor sets completed on merge to main
    51→
    52→When commits land on the main/default branch (via merge), set referenced tasks → completed. This is the "verified/merged" signal for teams.
    53→
    54→### R7: Keep server-owned backward transitions
    55→
    56→Abort and reject continue to reset task status on the server side:
    57→- Abort → ready
    58→- Reject → ready
    59→
    60→No changes needed here.
    61→
    62→## Setup-Dependent Behavior
    63→
    64→| Setup | in_progress | done | completed |
    65→|---|---|---|---|
    66→| Solo/local | Agent (brain-start-task) | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual |
    67→| Team/remote | Agent (brain-start-task) | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge to main) |
    68→
    69→## Constraints
    70→
    71→- **commit-check must not block git**: The post-commit hook must never fail the git workflow. API errors are logged but exit 0. This is a fire-and-forget signal.
    72→- **Idempotent transitions**: Setting done on an already-done task, or completed on an already-completed task, is a no-op. Both commit-check and GitHub processor may fire for the same commit — that's fine.
    73→- **Task ref convention required**: Completion detection depends on `task:<id>` or `tasks: <id1>, <id2>` in commit messages. No task refs = no status change. This is by design — not every commit maps to a task.
    74→
    75→## Non-Requirements
    76→
    77→- No migration of existing task statuses. Schema & data migration policy: breaking changes, no backfills.
    78→- No new task status values. Existing statuses (open, todo, ready, in_progress, blocked, done, completed) are sufficient.
    79→

1→# User Stories: Task Status Ownership
     2→
     3→## US-1: Remove server-side in_progress on assignment
     4→
     5→**As** a developer using the orchestrator,
     6→**I want** the server to stop setting in_progress when creating an agent session,
     7→**So that** only the agent that actually starts work owns that transition.
     8→
     9→**Traces to**: Job 1 (Accurate Task Progress), Job 3 (Graceful Recovery)
    10→**Size**: S
    11→
    12→## US-2: Remove server-side done on session accept
    13→
    14→**As** a developer reviewing agent work,
    15→**I want** session accept to only affect session lifecycle (not task status),
    16→**So that** task completion is tied to actual code, not review approval.
    17→
    18→**Traces to**: Job 1 (Accurate Task Progress), Job 2 (Reliable Task Tracking)
    19→**Size**: S
    20→
    21→## US-3: Add brain commit-check CLI command
    22→
    23→**As** a solo developer committing code locally,
    24→**I want** a `brain commit-check` command that parses task refs from commit messages and sets tasks to done,
    25→**So that** task status updates automatically when I commit without needing a remote push.
    26→
    27→**Traces to**: Job 1 (Accurate Task Progress)
    28→**Size**: M
    29→
    30→## US-4: Wire commit-check as post-commit hook
    31→
    32→**As** a developer using Brain-managed repositories,
    33→**I want** `brain commit-check` to run automatically as a git post-commit hook,
    34→**So that** I don't have to remember to run it manually after every commit.
    35→
    36→**Traces to**: Job 1 (Accurate Task Progress)
    37→**Size**: S
    38→
    39→## US-5: GitHub processor sets done on push
    40→
    41→**As** a team lead with agents pushing to remote branches,
    42→**I want** the GitHub commit processor to set referenced tasks to done when commits are pushed,
    43→**So that** the team can see which tasks have finished work pending review.
    44→
    45→**Traces to**: Job 2 (Reliable Task Tracking)
    46→**Size**: S
    47→
    48→## US-6: GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main
    49→
    50→**As** a team lead tracking shipped work,
    51→**I want** tasks to be marked completed when their commits merge to main,
    52→**So that** "completed" means the code is verified and landed — not just that an agent finished.
    53→
    54→**Traces to**: Job 2 (Reliable Task Tracking)
    55→**Size**: M
    56→

1→# Acceptance Criteria: Task Status Ownership
     2→
     3→## US-1: Remove server-side in_progress on assignment
     4→
     5→```gherkin
     6→Feature: Agent session creation does not change task status
     7→
     8→  Scenario: Creating agent session with a ready task
     9→    Given a task with status "ready"
    10→    When an agent session is created for that task
    11→    Then the task status remains "ready"
    12→    And the task source_session is linked to the new session
    13→
    14→  Scenario: Creating agent session with a todo task
    15→    Given a task with status "todo"
    16→    When an agent session is created for that task
    17→    Then the task status remains "todo"
    18→    And the task source_session is linked to the new session
    19→```
    20→
    21→## US-2: Remove server-side done on session accept
    22→
    23→```gherkin
    24→Feature: Session accept does not change task status
    25→
    26→  Scenario: Accepting a session with task in in_progress
    27→    Given an agent session linked to a task with status "in_progress"
    28→    When the session is accepted
    29→    Then the session status becomes "accepted"
    30→    And the task status remains "in_progress"
    31→
    32→  Scenario: Accepting a session with task already done
    33→    Given an agent session linked to a task with status "done"
    34→    When the session is accepted
    35→    Then the session status becomes "accepted"
    36→    And the task status remains "done"
    37→```
    38→
    39→## US-3: Add brain commit-check CLI command
    40→
    41→```gherkin
    42→Feature: brain commit-check parses task refs and sets done
    43→
    44→  Scenario: Commit message with single task ref
    45→    Given a commit with message "Implement login flow\n\ntask:abc123"
    46→    When brain commit-check runs
    47→    Then task "abc123" status is set to "done"
    48→
    49→  Scenario: Commit message with multiple task refs
    50→    Given a commit with message "Batch update\n\ntasks: abc123, def456"
    51→    When brain commit-check runs
    52→    Then task "abc123" status is set to "done"
    53→    And task "def456" status is set to "done"
    54→
    55→  Scenario: Commit message with no task refs
    56→    Given a commit with message "Fix typo in README"
    57→    When brain commit-check runs
    58→    Then no task status changes occur
    59→
    60→  Scenario: Task already done (idempotent)
    61→    Given a commit referencing task "abc123"
    62→    And task "abc123" already has status "done"
    63→    When brain commit-check runs
    64→    Then task "abc123" status remains "done"
    65→    And no error is raised
    66→
    67→  Scenario: Commit check does not block the git workflow
    68→    When brain commit-check runs as a post-commit hook
    69→    Then it completes without blocking subsequent git operations
    70→    And API failures are logged but do not cause a non-zero exit code
    71→```
    72→
    73→## US-4: Wire commit-check as post-commit hook
    74→
    75→```gherkin
    76→Feature: commit-check runs as post-commit hook
    77→
    78→  Scenario: Git commit triggers commit-check
    79→    Given a Brain-managed repository with post-commit hook installed
    80→    When a developer makes a git commit with message containing "task:abc123"
    81→    Then brain commit-check runs automatically after the commit
    82→    And task "abc123" status is set to "done"
    83→```
    84→
    85→## US-5: GitHub processor sets done on push
    86→
    87→```gherkin
    88→Feature: GitHub commit processor sets done on push to non-main branch
    89→
    90→  Scenario: Push to feature branch with task refs
    91→    Given a commit pushed to branch "feature/login" with message "task:abc123"
    92→    When the GitHub commit processor runs
    93→    Then task "abc123" status is set to "done"
    94→    And an implemented_by relation is created
    95→
    96→  Scenario: Push to main branch
    97→    Given a commit pushed to branch "main" with message "task:abc123"
    98→    When the GitHub commit processor runs
    99→    Then task "abc123" status is set to "completed"
   100→    And an implemented_by relation is created
   101→```
   102→
   103→## US-6: GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main
   104→
   105→```gherkin
   106→Feature: GitHub commit processor sets completed on merge
   107→
   108→  Scenario: PR merged to main with task refs
   109→    Given commits merged to "main" referencing "task:abc123"
   110→    When the GitHub commit processor runs for the merge
   111→    Then task "abc123" status is set to "completed"
   112→
   113→  Scenario: Task already completed (idempotent)
   114→    Given a merge to main referencing "task:abc123"
   115→    And task "abc123" already has status "completed"
   116→    When the GitHub commit processor runs
   117→    Then task "abc123" status remains "completed"
   118→```
   119→
   120→## Backward transitions (unchanged, for reference)
   121→
   122→```gherkin
   123→Feature: Server owns backward transitions
   124→
   125→  Scenario: Session aborted resets task to ready
   126→    Given an agent session linked to a task with status "in_progress"
   127→    When the session is aborted
   128→    Then the task status is set to "ready"
   129→
   130→  Scenario: Session rejected resets task to ready
   131→    Given an agent session linked to a task with status "in_progress"
   132→    When the session is rejected
   133→    Then the task status is set to "ready"
   134→```
   135→

    26→
    27→1. **Decisions**: Implementation choices made but not logged. Call create_provisional_decision for each.
    28→2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
    29→3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
    30→4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
    31→5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
    32→6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).
    33→
    34→After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
    35→{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}
    36→
    37→If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
    38→{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}`,
    39→    },
    40→  ],
    41→  SessionEnd: [
    42→    { type: "command", command: "brain system end-session" },
    43→  ],
    44→};

1→# JTBD Job Stories: Task Status Ownership
     2→
     3→## Job 1: Accurate Task Progress (Solo Developer)
     4→
     5→**Job Story**: When I'm working solo on tasks using Claude Code locally, I want task status to reflect what's actually happening (agent started, code committed), so I can trust the task board without manually updating statuses.
     6→
     7→### Dimensions
     8→- **Functional**: Task status updates automatically based on real events (agent start, commit)
     9→- **Emotional**: Confidence that the board reflects reality without babysitting
    10→- **Social**: N/A (solo context)
    11→
    12→## Job 2: Reliable Task Tracking (Team Lead)
    13→
    14→**Job Story**: When my team has multiple agents working on tasks across branches, I want task completion to be tied to actual code landing in main, so I can trust that "completed" means verified and merged — not just that an agent said it finished.
    15→
    16→### Dimensions
    17→- **Functional**: Merge-to-main triggers completed status; push triggers done (pending review)
    18→- **Emotional**: Trust that status reflects shipped reality, not agent optimism
    19→- **Social**: Team can rely on task board as source of truth in standups
    20→
    21→## Job 3: Graceful Recovery (Orchestrator Operator)
    22→
    23→**Job Story**: When an agent crashes or produces rejected work, I want the task to automatically become available again, so I don't have stuck tasks blocking the pipeline.
    24→
    25→### Dimensions
    26→- **Functional**: Abort/reject resets task to "ready" so another agent can pick it up
    27→- **Emotional**: No anxiety about orphaned in-progress tasks
    28→- **Social**: Team doesn't see phantom "in progress" work
    29→

1→---
     2→name: critique-dimensions
     3→description: Architecture quality critique dimensions for peer review. Load when invoking solution-architect-reviewer or performing self-review of architecture documents.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Architecture Quality Critique Dimensions
     7→
     8→## Dimension 1: Architectural Bias Detection
     9→
    10→### Technology Preference Bias
    11→Pattern: tech chosen by preference, not requirements. Detection: ADR lacks comparison matrix, choice not mapped to requirements, justified only as "best practice." Severity: HIGH.
    12→
    13→### Resume-Driven Development
    14→Pattern: complex/trendy tech without requirement justification. Examples: microservices for 3-person team, Kafka for 100 req/day, service mesh without complexity. Detection: complexity exceeds team size/requirements, tech adds resume value not solves problem. Severity: CRITICAL.
    15→
    16→### Latest Technology Bias
    17→Pattern: unproven tech (<6 months, small community) for production. Detection: check maturity, community, LTS, fallback plan. Severity: HIGH.
    18→
    19→## Dimension 2: ADR Quality Validation
    20→
    21→### Missing Context
    22→ADR lacks business problem, technical constraints, or quality attribute requirements. Future maintainers cannot validate. Severity: HIGH.
    23→
    24→### Missing Alternatives Analysis
    25→No alternatives (min 2 required). Each must be evaluated against requirements with rejection rationale. Severity: HIGH.
    26→
    27→### Missing Consequences
    28→Omits positive/negative consequences and trade-offs. Quality attribute impact not analyzed. Severity: MEDIUM.
    29→
    30→## Dimension 3: Completeness Validation
    31→
    32→### Missing Quality Attributes
    33→Architecture doesn't address required attributes. Verify: performance (latency, throughput) | scalability | security (auth, data protection) | maintainability (modularity, testability) | reliability (fault tolerance, recovery) | observability (logging, monitoring, alerting). Severity: CRITICAL.
    34→
    35→### Missing Performance Architecture
    36→Performance requirements exist but no optimization strategy (caching, indexing, rate limiting, CDN). Severity: CRITICAL.
    37→
    38→## Dimension 4: Implementation Feasibility
    39→
    40→### Team Capability Mismatch
    41→Requires expertise team lacks. Verify learning curve reasonable, training plan exists. Severity: HIGH.
    42→
    43→### Budget Constraints
    44→Infrastructure costs exceed budget. Verify cost estimate exists and aligns. Severity: HIGH.
    45→
    46→### Testability Validation
    47→Architecture prevents effective testing. Components must enable isolated testing with ports/adapters. Severity: CRITICAL.
    48→
    49→## Dimension 5: Priority Validation
    50→
    51→Validate roadmap addresses largest bottleneck.
    52→
    53→**Q1**: Largest bottleneck? (timing data must confirm primary problem)
    54→**Q2**: Simpler alternatives considered? (rejected alternatives required)
    55→**Q3**: Constraint prioritization correct? (quantified by impact, constraint-free first)
    56→**Q4**: Data-justified? (key decision with quantitative data)
    57→
    58→Failure: Q1=NO (wrong problem) | Q2=MISSING (no alternatives) | Q3=INVERTED (>50% solution for <30% problem) | Q4=NO_DATA for performance
    59→
    60→## Review Output Format
    61→
    62→```yaml
    63→review_id: "arch_rev_{timestamp}"
    64→reviewer: "solution-architect-reviewer"
    65→artifact: "docs/architecture/architecture.md, docs/adrs/*.md"
    66→iteration: {1 or 2}
    67→
    68→strengths:
    69→  - "{Positive decision with ADR reference}"
    70→
    71→issues_identified:
    72→  architectural_bias:
    73→    - issue: "{pattern detected}"
    74→      severity: "critical|high|medium|low"
    75→      location: "{ADR or section}"
    76→      recommendation: "{actionable fix}"
    77→  decision_quality:
    78→    - issue: "{ADR quality issue}"
    79→      severity: "high"
    80→      location: "ADR-{number}"
    81→      recommendation: "{add missing section}"
    82→  completeness_gaps:
    83→    - issue: "{quality attribute not addressed}"
    84→      severity: "critical"
    85→      recommendation: "{add architecture section}"
    86→  implementation_feasibility:
    87→    - issue: "{capability, budget, testability concern}"
    88→      severity: "high"
    89→      recommendation: "{simplify or add mitigation}"
    90→  priority_validation:
    91→    q1_largest_bottleneck:
    92→      evidence: "{data or NOT PROVIDED}"
    93→      assessment: "YES|NO|UNCLEAR"
    94→    q2_simple_alternatives:
    95→      assessment: "ADEQUATE|INADEQUATE|MISSING"
    96→    q3_constraint_prioritization:
    97→      assessment: "CORRECT|INVERTED|NOT_ANALYZED"
    98→    q4_data_justified:
    99→      assessment: "JUSTIFIED|UNJUSTIFIED|NO_DATA"
   100→
   101→approval_status: "approved|rejected_pending_revisions|conditionally_approved"
   102→critical_issues_count: {number}
   103→high_issues_count: {number}
   104→```
   105→
   106→## Severity Classification
   107→
   108→- **Critical**: resume-driven dev, missing critical quality attributes, untestable, wrong problem
   109→- **High**: technology bias, incomplete ADRs, feasibility concerns, missing data
   110→- **Medium**: missing consequences, minor completeness gaps
   111→- **Low**: documentation improvements, naming consistency

input.content:
# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership

## System Context

Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned.

### Capabilities

| Capability | Owner | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool |
| done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages |
| completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected |
| ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` |
| ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` |

### Setup-Dependent Behavior

| Setup | in_progress | done | completed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual |
| Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) |

## C4 System Context (L1)

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer", "Commits code locally")
    Person(lead, "Team Lead", "Reviews PRs, monitors task board")

    System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph with task lifecycle management")

    System_Ext(github, "GitHub", "Remote git hosting, webhooks")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent", "AI coding agent with MCP tools")

    Rel(dev, brain, "Commits trigger status updates via", "post-commit hook")
    Rel(claude, brain, "Sets in_progress via", "MCP update_task_status")
    Rel(github, brain, "Pushes trigger done/completed via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(dev, github, "Pushes commits to", "git push")
    Rel(lead, brain, "Views task status via", "Web UI")
```

## C4 Container (L2)

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Container: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent")

    System_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(cli, "Brain CLI", "Bun binary", "commit-check command, git hooks, MCP server")
        Container(server, "Brain Server", "Bun HTTP", "API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator")
        ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "SurrealDB v3", "Tasks, sessions, git_commits, relations")
    }

    Rel(claude, cli, "Calls tools via", "MCP stdio")
    Rel(cli, server, "Calls API via", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(dev, cli, "Triggers via", "git post-commit hook")
    Rel(github, server, "Sends push events via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(server, surreal, "Reads/writes via", "SurrealDB SDK")
    Rel(cli, surreal, "No direct access", "")
```

## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Component: Task Status Transition Ownership

    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Agent")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    Person(dev, "Developer")

    Container_Boundary(cli_boundary, "Brain CLI") {
        Component(commit_check, "commit-check", "CLI command", "Parses task refs from commit, calls API to set done")
        Component(post_commit_hook, "post-commit hook", "Shell script", "Invokes brain commit-check fire-and-forget")
        Component(mcp_server, "MCP Server", "stdio", "Exposes update_task_status tool")
    }

    Container_Boundary(server_boundary, "Brain Server") {
        Component(mcp_route, "MCP Route", "HTTP handler", "Receives task status updates from CLI")
        Component(session_lifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Module", "Manages abort/reject backward transitions")
        Component(create_session, "createAgentSession", "Function", "Links task to session WITHOUT status change")
        Component(webhook_handler, "GitHub Webhook Handler", "HTTP handler", "Receives push events")
        Component(commit_processor, "Commit Processor", "Module", "Parses task refs, sets done or completed")
        Component(task_ref_parser, "commit-task-refs", "Pure function", "Regex extraction of task:id from messages")
    }

    Rel(claude, mcp_server, "Calls update_task_status", "MCP")
    Rel(mcp_server, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(dev, post_commit_hook, "Triggers on commit", "git hook")
    Rel(post_commit_hook, commit_check, "Invokes", "shell exec")
    Rel(commit_check, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(github, webhook_handler, "Sends push event", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(webhook_handler, commit_processor, "Delegates to", "async")
    Rel(commit_processor, task_ref_parser, "Extracts task IDs from", "function call")
    Rel(session_lifecycle, create_session, "No longer sets in_progress", "removed")
```

## Component Architecture

### Changes by Module

#### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update
- **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record
- **Keep**: Workspace scope validation
- **Traces to**: US-1, R1

#### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task
- **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session
- **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup
- **Traces to**: US-2, R1

#### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck()
- **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`)
- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
- **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error
- **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`
- **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts`
- **Traces to**: US-3, R3

#### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation
- **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook
- **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check)
- **Traces to**: US-4, R4

#### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing
- **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias)
- **Traces to**: US-3, US-4

#### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit()
- **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch:
  - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done`
  - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed`
- **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch`
- **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6

#### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook()
- **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`)
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

#### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult
- **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts

The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun.

**No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`).

### Idempotent Status Transitions

Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers.

### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution)

| Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done |
| Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done |
| Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed |
| Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed |

## Technology Stack

No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack:

| Component | Technology | License | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime |
| Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime |
| Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store |
| Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists |
| HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists |

## Integration Patterns

### CLI to Server (commit-check)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status`
- **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow)
- **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }`
- **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0.

### GitHub to Server (webhook)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing)
- **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler)
- **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}`
- **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done`

### Agent to Server (in_progress)
- **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing)
- **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes)
- **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes)

## Quality Attribute Strategies

### Reliability
- **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor
- **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow
- **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged

### Maintainability
- **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source
- **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication
- **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists

### Observability
- **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA
- **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking

### Testability
- **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation
- **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient`
- **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions

## Deployment Architecture

No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

input.content:
# ADR-009: Task Status Transition Ownership Model

## Status

Proposed

## Context

Task status transitions are duplicated between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors:

- `createAgentSession()` sets `in_progress` when a session is created for a task, but the agent also sets `in_progress` via `brain-start-task` -> `update_task_status`. The server transition is optimistic and fires even if the agent never starts work.
- `acceptOrchestratorSession()` sets `done` when a session is accepted, but this conflates review approval with code completion. A task can have committed code (done) before session review.

This creates orphaned states on agent crashes (task stuck in `in_progress` with no active session) and semantic confusion (what does "done" mean -- code committed or session reviewed?).

Quality attribute drivers: **reliability** (accurate status), **maintainability** (single ownership per transition), **operational simplicity** (no manual status cleanup).

## Decision

Forward transitions are owned by the entity doing the work. Backward transitions are owned by the server (orchestrator).

| Transition | Owner | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| -> in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` calls `update_task_status` |
| -> done | commit-check (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Commit with task refs |
| -> completed | GitHub processor | Merge to default branch |
| -> ready (abort) | Server | Session aborted |
| -> ready (reject) | Server | Session rejected |

Transitions are idempotent and forward-only: setting `done` on an already-`done` or `completed` task is a no-op.

## Alternatives Considered

### Alternative 1: Keep server-owned forward transitions, add deduplication

- **What**: Keep `in_progress` in `createAgentSession()` and `done` in `acceptOrchestratorSession()`, add idempotency guards so agent/processor transitions are no-ops when server already set the status.
- **Expected Impact**: Solves duplication conflicts (~60% of problem).
- **Why Insufficient**: Does not fix semantic confusion. `done` still means "session accepted" rather than "code committed." Orphaned `in_progress` on agent crash still possible because server sets it before agent starts.

### Alternative 2: Event sourcing for status transitions

- **What**: Record all transition events with source attribution; derive current status from event log.
- **Expected Impact**: Full audit trail (~100% of problem).
- **Why Insufficient**: Massive complexity increase for a simple ownership problem. Team size and system maturity do not justify event sourcing infrastructure. Violates simplest-solution-first principle.

### Alternative 3: Server as sole authority with agent status reports

- **What**: Only the server sets status. Agents report progress events; server decides transitions.
- **Expected Impact**: Single authority (~80% of problem).
- **Why Insufficient**: Adds indirection. The commit-check hook would need to report "commit happened" and wait for server to decide status. Increases latency and coupling for no gain -- the mapping from "commit with task ref" to "done" is deterministic.

## Consequences

### Positive

- Each transition has exactly one authoritative source -- no duplication
- `done` semantically means "code committed," `completed` means "merged to main"
- Agent crash does not orphan tasks (server never set `in_progress`)
- Solo developers get immediate feedback via post-commit hook
- Teams get dual coverage (local hook + GitHub webhook)

### Negative

- `in_progress` depends on agent calling `update_task_status` -- if agent skips `brain-start-task`, task stays in previous status
- `done` depends on task refs in commit messages -- commits without `task:id` do not trigger status change
- Small window where commit-check and GitHub processor may both attempt `done` (resolved by idempotency)

          - "Accepting session does not change linked task status"
          - "Session orchestrator_status still becomes completed"

      "02-02":

          - "brain init creates post-commit hook that invokes brain commit-check"

  "03":
    title: "GitHub processor status transitions"
    steps:
      "03-01":
        title: "GitHub processor sets done on non-main push"

          - "Push to feature branch with task refs sets tasks to done"
          - "implemented_by relation is still created"

        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify processCommit() in github-commit-processor.ts"
          - "Branch info from GitHubPushEvent.ref vs repository.default_branch"
          - "Idempotent: skip if task already at or beyond target status"
        traces: [US-5, R5]

      "03-02":
        title: "GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main"
        description: "When push targets the default branch, set referenced tasks to completed instead of done"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Merge/push to main with task refs sets tasks to completed"
          - "Already-completed tasks remain completed without error"
          - "Non-main branch pushes still set done (not completed)"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Same processCommit() modification, branching on isDefaultBranch"
          - "Extend ProcessWebhookResult with taskStatusUpdates for observability"
        traces: [US-6, R6]

    - path: "app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts"

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership

## System Context

Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned.

### Capabilities

| Capability | Owner | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool |
| done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages |
| completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected |
| ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` |
| ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` |

### Setup-Dependent Behavior

| Setup | in_progress | done | completed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual |
| Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) |

## C4 System Context (L1)

```mermaid
C4Context
    title System Context: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer", "Commits code locally")
    Person(lead, "Team Lead", "Reviews PRs, monitors task board")

    System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph with task lifecycle management")

    System_Ext(github, "GitHub", "Remote git hosting, webhooks")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent", "AI coding agent with MCP tools")

    Rel(dev, brain, "Commits trigger status updates via", "post-commit hook")
    Rel(claude, brain, "Sets in_progress via", "MCP update_task_status")
    Rel(github, brain, "Pushes trigger done/completed via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(dev, github, "Pushes commits to", "git push")
    Rel(lead, brain, "Views task status via", "Web UI")
```

## C4 Container (L2)

```mermaid
C4Container
    title Container: Task Status Ownership

    Person(dev, "Developer")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent")

    System_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
        Container(cli, "Brain CLI", "Bun binary", "commit-check command, git hooks, MCP server")
        Container(server, "Brain Server", "Bun HTTP", "API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator")
        ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "SurrealDB v3", "Tasks, sessions, git_commits, relations")
    }

    Rel(claude, cli, "Calls tools via", "MCP stdio")
    Rel(cli, server, "Calls API via", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(dev, cli, "Triggers via", "git post-commit hook")
    Rel(github, server, "Sends push events via", "Webhook POST")
    Rel(server, surreal, "Reads/writes via", "SurrealDB SDK")
    Rel(cli, surreal, "No direct access", "")
```

## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Component: Task Status Transition Ownership

    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Agent")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    Person(dev, "Developer")

    Container_Boundary(cli_boundary, "Brain CLI") {
        Component(commit_check, "commit-check", "CLI command", "Parses task refs from commit, calls API to set done")
        Component(post_commit_hook, "post-commit hook", "Shell script", "Invokes brain commit-check fire-and-forget")
        Component(mcp_server, "MCP Server", "stdio", "Exposes update_task_status tool")
    }

    Container_Boundary(server_boundary, "Brain Server") {
        Component(mcp_route, "MCP Route", "HTTP handler", "Receives task status updates from CLI")
        Component(session_lifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Module", "Manages abort/reject backward transitions")
        Component(create_session, "createAgentSession", "Function", "Links task to session WITHOUT status change")
        Component(webhook_handler, "GitHub Webhook Handler", "HTTP handler", "Receives push events")
        Component(commit_processor, "Commit Processor", "Module", "Parses task refs, sets done or completed")
        Component(task_ref_parser, "commit-task-refs", "Pure function", "Regex extraction of task:id from messages")
    }

    Rel(claude, mcp_server, "Calls update_task_status", "MCP")
    Rel(mcp_server, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(dev, post_commit_hook, "Triggers on commit", "git hook")
    Rel(post_commit_hook, commit_check, "Invokes", "shell exec")
    Rel(commit_check, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(github, webhook_handler, "Sends push event", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(webhook_handler, commit_processor, "Delegates to", "async")
    Rel(commit_processor, task_ref_parser, "Extracts task IDs from", "function call")
    Rel(session_lifecycle, create_session, "No longer sets in_progress", "removed")
```

## Component Architecture

### Changes by Module

#### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update
- **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record
- **Keep**: Workspace scope validation
- **Traces to**: US-1, R1

#### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task
- **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session
- **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup
- **Traces to**: US-2, R1

#### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck()
- **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`)
- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
- **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error
- **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`
- **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts`
- **Traces to**: US-3, R3

#### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation
- **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook
- **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check)
- **Traces to**: US-4, R4

#### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing
- **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias)
- **Traces to**: US-3, US-4

#### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit()
- **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch:
  - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done`
  - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed`
- **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch`
- **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6

#### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook()
- **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`)
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

#### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult
- **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts

The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun.

**No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`).

### Idempotent Status Transitions

Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers.

### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution)

| Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done |
| Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done |
| Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed |
| Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed |

## Technology Stack

No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack:

| Component | Technology | License | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime |
| Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime |
| Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store |
| Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists |
| HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists |

## Integration Patterns

### CLI to Server (commit-check)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status`
- **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow)
- **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }`
- **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0.

### GitHub to Server (webhook)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing)
- **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler)
- **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}`
- **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done`

### Agent to Server (in_progress)
- **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing)
- **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes)
- **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes)

## Quality Attribute Strategies

### Reliability
- **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor
- **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow
- **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged

### Maintainability
- **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source
- **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication
- **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists

### Observability
- **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA
- **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking

### Testability
- **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation
- **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient`
- **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions

## Deployment Architecture

No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).
