# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership ## System Context Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned. ### Capabilities | Capability | Owner | Mechanism | |---|---|---| | in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool | | done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages | | completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected | | ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` | | ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` | ### Setup-Dependent Behavior | Setup | in_progress | done | completed | |---|---|---|---| | Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual | | Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) | ## C4 System Context (L1) ```mermaid graph TB dev["👤 Developer
Commits code locally"] lead["👤 Team Lead
Reviews PRs, monitors task board"] claude["🤖 Claude Code Agent
AI coding agent with MCP tools"] github["☁️ GitHub
Remote git hosting, webhooks"] brain["🧠 Brain Platform
Knowledge graph with task lifecycle"] dev -->|"post-commit hook"| brain dev -->|"git push"| github claude -->|"MCP update_task_status
→ in_progress"| brain github -->|"Webhook POST
→ done / completed"| brain lead -->|"Web UI"| brain style brain fill:#1168bd,stroke:#0b4884,color:#fff style github fill:#999,stroke:#666,color:#fff style claude fill:#999,stroke:#666,color:#fff ``` ## C4 Container (L2) ```mermaid C4Container title Container: Task Status Ownership Person(dev, "Developer") System_Ext(github, "GitHub") System_Ext(claude, "Claude Code Agent") System_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") { Container(cli, "Brain CLI", "Bun binary", "commit-check command, git hooks, MCP server") Container(server, "Brain Server", "Bun HTTP", "API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator") ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "SurrealDB v3", "Tasks, sessions, git_commits, relations") } Rel(claude, cli, "Calls tools via", "MCP stdio") Rel(cli, server, "Calls API via", "HTTP POST") Rel(dev, cli, "Triggers via", "git post-commit hook") Rel(github, server, "Sends push events via", "Webhook POST") Rel(server, surreal, "Reads/writes via", "SurrealDB SDK") Rel(cli, surreal, "No direct access", "") ``` ## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow ```mermaid C4Component title Component: Task Status Transition Ownership System_Ext(claude, "Claude Agent") System_Ext(github, "GitHub") Person(dev, "Developer") Container_Boundary(cli_boundary, "Brain CLI") { Component(commit_check, "commit-check", "CLI command", "Parses task refs from commit, calls API to set done") Component(post_commit_hook, "post-commit hook", "Shell script", "Invokes brain commit-check fire-and-forget") Component(mcp_server, "MCP Server", "stdio", "Exposes update_task_status tool") } Container_Boundary(server_boundary, "Brain Server") { Component(mcp_route, "MCP Route", "HTTP handler", "Receives task status updates from CLI") Component(session_lifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Module", "Manages abort/reject backward transitions") Component(create_session, "createAgentSession", "Function", "Links task to session WITHOUT status change") Component(webhook_handler, "GitHub Webhook Handler", "HTTP handler", "Receives push events") Component(commit_processor, "Commit Processor", "Module", "Parses task refs, sets done or completed") Component(task_ref_parser, "commit-task-refs", "Pure function", "Regex extraction of task:id from messages") } Rel(claude, mcp_server, "Calls update_task_status", "MCP") Rel(mcp_server, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status") Rel(dev, post_commit_hook, "Triggers on commit", "git hook") Rel(post_commit_hook, commit_check, "Invokes", "shell exec") Rel(commit_check, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status") Rel(github, webhook_handler, "Sends push event", "HTTP POST") Rel(webhook_handler, commit_processor, "Delegates to", "async") Rel(commit_processor, task_ref_parser, "Extracts task IDs from", "function call") Rel(session_lifecycle, create_session, "No longer sets in_progress", "removed") ``` ## Component Architecture ### Changes by Module #### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession() - **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update - **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record - **Keep**: Workspace scope validation - **Traces to**: US-1, R1 #### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession() - **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task - **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session - **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup - **Traces to**: US-2, R1 #### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck() - **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`) - **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each - **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error - **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` - **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts` - **Traces to**: US-3, R3 #### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation - **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook - **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check) - **Traces to**: US-4, R4 #### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing - **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias) - **Traces to**: US-3, US-4 #### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit() - **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch: - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done` - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed` - **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch` - **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6 #### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook() - **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`) - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 #### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult - **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 ### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun. **No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`). ### Idempotent Status Transitions Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers. ### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution) | Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result | |---|---|---|---| | Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done | | Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done | | Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed | | Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed | ## Technology Stack No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack: | Component | Technology | License | Rationale | |---|---|---|---| | CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime | | Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime | | Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store | | Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists | | HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists | ## Integration Patterns ### CLI to Server (commit-check) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status` - **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow) - **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }` - **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0. ### GitHub to Server (webhook) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing) - **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler) - **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}` - **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done` ### Agent to Server (in_progress) - **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing) - **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes) - **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes) ## Quality Attribute Strategies ### Reliability - **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor - **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow - **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged ### Maintainability - **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source - **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication - **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists ### Observability - **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA - **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking ### Testability - **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation - **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient` - **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions ## Deployment Architecture No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

Bun binary

Bun HTTP
API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator"]

input.old_string:
## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow

```mermaid
C4Component
    title Component: Task Status Transition Ownership

    System_Ext(claude, "Claude Agent")
    System_Ext(github, "GitHub")
    Person(dev, "Developer")

    Container_Boundary(cli_boundary, "Brain CLI") {
        Component(commit_check, "commit-check", "CLI command", "Parses task refs from commit, calls API to set done")
        Component(post_commit_hook, "post-commit hook", "Shell script", "Invokes brain commit-check fire-and-forget")
        Component(mcp_server, "MCP Server", "stdio", "Exposes update_task_status tool")
    }

    Container_Boundary(server_boundary, "Brain Server") {
        Component(mcp_route, "MCP Route", "HTTP handler", "Receives task status updates from CLI")
        Component(session_lifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Module", "Manages abort/reject backward transitions")
        Component(create_session, "createAgentSession", "Function", "Links task to session WITHOUT status change")
        Component(webhook_handler, "GitHub Webhook Handler", "HTTP handler", "Receives push events")
        Component(commit_processor, "Commit Processor", "Module", "Parses task refs, sets done or completed")
        Component(task_ref_parser, "commit-task-refs", "Pure function", "Regex extraction of task:id from messages")
    }

    Rel(claude, mcp_server, "Calls update_task_status", "MCP")
    Rel(mcp_server, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(dev, post_commit_hook, "Triggers on commit", "git hook")
    Rel(post_commit_hook, commit_check, "Invokes", "shell exec")
    Rel(commit_check, mcp_route, "HTTP POST", "/tasks/status")
    Rel(github, webhook_handler, "Sends push event", "HTTP POST")
    Rel(webhook_handler, commit_processor, "Delegates to", "async")
    Rel(commit_processor, task_ref_parser, "Extracts task IDs from", "function call")
    Rel(session_lifecycle, create_session, "No longer sets in_progress", "removed")
```

This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers. ### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution) | Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result | |---|---|---|---| | Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done | | Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done | | Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed | | Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed | ## Technology Stack No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack: | Component | Technology | License | Rationale | |---|---|---|---| | CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime | | Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime | | Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store | | Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists | | HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists | ## Integration Patterns ### CLI to Server (commit-check) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status` - **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow) - **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }` - **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0. ### GitHub to Server (webhook) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing) - **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler) - **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}` - **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done` ### Agent to Server (in_progress) - **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing) - **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes) - **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes) ## Quality Attribute Strategies ### Reliability - **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor - **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow - **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged ### Maintainability - **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source - **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication - **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists ### Observability - **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA - **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking ### Testability - **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation - **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient` - **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions ## Deployment Architecture No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership ## System Context Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned. ### Capabilities | Capability | Owner | Mechanism | |---|---|---| | in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool | | done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages | | completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected | | ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` | | ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` | ### Setup-Dependent Behavior | Setup | in_progress | done | completed | |---|---|---|---| | Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual | | Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) | ## C4 System Context (L1) ```mermaid graph TB dev["👤 Developer
Commits code locally"] lead["👤 Team Lead
Reviews PRs, monitors task board"] claude["🤖 Claude Code Agent
AI coding agent with MCP tools"] github["☁️ GitHub
Remote git hosting, webhooks"] brain["🧠 Brain Platform
Knowledge graph with task lifecycle"] dev -->|"post-commit hook"| brain dev -->|"git push"| github claude -->|"MCP update_task_status
→ in_progress"| brain github -->|"Webhook POST
→ done / completed"| brain lead -->|"Web UI"| brain style brain fill:#1168bd,stroke:#0b4884,color:#fff style github fill:#999,stroke:#666,color:#fff style claude fill:#999,stroke:#666,color:#fff ``` ## C4 Container (L2) ```mermaid graph TB dev["👤 Developer"] github["☁️ GitHub"] claude["🤖 Claude Code Agent"] subgraph brain ["Brain Platform"] cli["📦 Brain CLI
Bun binary
commit-check, git hooks, MCP server"] server["📦 Brain Server
Bun HTTP
API routes, webhook handler, orchestrator"] surreal[("🗄️ SurrealDB v3
Tasks, sessions, relations")] end claude -->|"MCP stdio"| cli cli -->|"HTTP POST"| server dev -->|"post-commit hook"| cli github -->|"Webhook POST"| server server -->|"SurrealDB SDK"| surreal style brain fill:#e8e8e8,stroke:#999 style cli fill:#438dd5,stroke:#2e6295,color:#fff style server fill:#438dd5,stroke:#2e6295,color:#fff style surreal fill:#438dd5,stroke:#2e6295,color:#fff ``` ## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow ```mermaid graph TB claude["🤖 Claude Agent"] github["☁️ GitHub"] dev["👤 Developer"] subgraph cli_boundary ["Brain CLI"] post_commit_hook["post-commit hook
Shell script"] commit_check["commit-check
CLI command
Parse task refs → API call → done"] mcp_server["MCP Server
stdio
update_task_status tool"] end subgraph server_boundary ["Brain Server"] mcp_route["MCP Route
HTTP handler
Receives status updates"] session_lifecycle["Session Lifecycle
Module
abort/reject → ready"] create_session["createAgentSession
Function
Links task to session
NO status change"] webhook_handler["Webhook Handler
HTTP handler
Receives push events"] commit_processor["Commit Processor
Module
Sets done or completed"] task_ref_parser["commit-task-refs
Pure function
Regex task:id extraction"] end claude -->|"MCP"| mcp_server mcp_server -->|"HTTP POST /tasks/status"| mcp_route dev -->|"git commit"| post_commit_hook post_commit_hook -->|"shell exec"| commit_check commit_check -->|"HTTP POST /tasks/status"| mcp_route github -->|"Webhook POST"| webhook_handler webhook_handler -->|"async"| commit_processor commit_processor --> task_ref_parser session_lifecycle -.->|"removed"| create_session style cli_boundary fill:#e8e8e8,stroke:#999 style server_boundary fill:#e8e8e8,stroke:#999 style create_session fill:#f9f,stroke:#c0c,color:#333 ``` ## Component Architecture ### Changes by Module #### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession() - **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update - **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record - **Keep**: Workspace scope validation - **Traces to**: US-1, R1 #### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession() - **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task - **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session - **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup - **Traces to**: US-2, R1 #### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck() - **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`) - **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each - **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error - **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` - **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts` - **Traces to**: US-3, R3 #### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation - **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook - **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check) - **Traces to**: US-4, R4 #### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing - **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias) - **Traces to**: US-3, US-4 #### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit() - **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch: - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done` - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed` - **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch` - **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6 #### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook() - **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`) - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 #### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult - **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 ### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun. **No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`). ### Idempotent Status Transitions Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers. ### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution) | Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result | |---|---|---|---| | Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done | | Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done | | Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed | | Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed | ## Technology Stack No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack: | Component | Technology | License | Rationale | |---|---|---|---| | CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime | | Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime | | Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store | | Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists | | HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists | ## Integration Patterns ### CLI to Server (commit-check) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status` - **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow) - **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }` - **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0. ### GitHub to Server (webhook) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing) - **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler) - **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}` - **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done` ### Agent to Server (in_progress) - **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing) - **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes) - **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes) ## Quality Attribute Strategies ### Reliability - **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor - **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow - **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged ### Maintainability - **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source - **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication - **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists ### Observability - **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA - **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking ### Testability - **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation - **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient` - **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions ## Deployment Architecture No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

```mermaid
        post_commit_hook[post-commit hook]
        commit_check[commit-check command]
        mcp_server[MCP Server]
    end

    subgraph server_boundary [Brain Server]
        mcp_route[MCP Route]
        session_lifecycle[Session Lifecycle - abort/reject]
        create_session[createAgentSession - NO status change]
        webhook_handler[Webhook Handler]
        commit_processor[Commit Processor - done/completed]
        task_ref_parser[commit-task-refs - pure function]
    end

    claude -->|MCP| mcp_server
    mcp_server -->|HTTP POST /tasks/status| mcp_route
    dev -->|git commit| post_commit_hook
    post_commit_hook -->|shell exec| commit_check
    commit_check -->|HTTP POST /tasks/status| mcp_route
    github -->|Webhook POST| webhook_handler
    webhook_handler -->|async| commit_processor
    commit_processor --> task_ref_parser
    session_lifecycle -.->|removed| create_session
```
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership ## System Context Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned. ### Capabilities | Capability | Owner | Mechanism | |---|---|---| | in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool | | done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages | | completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected | | ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` | | ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` | ### Setup-Dependent Behavior | Setup | in_progress | done | completed | |---|---|---|---| | Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual | | Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) | ## C4 System Context (L1) ```mermaid graph TB dev[Developer] lead[Team Lead] claude[Claude Code Agent] github[GitHub] brain[Brain Platform] dev -->|post-commit hook| brain dev -->|git push| github claude -->|MCP: in_progress| brain github -->|Webhook: done/completed| brain lead -->|Web UI| brain ``` ## C4 Container (L2) ```mermaid graph TB dev[Developer] github[GitHub] claude[Claude Code Agent] subgraph brain [Brain Platform] cli[Brain CLI - Bun binary] server[Brain Server - Bun HTTP] surreal[(SurrealDB v3)] end claude -->|MCP stdio| cli cli -->|HTTP POST| server dev -->|post-commit hook| cli github -->|Webhook POST| server server -->|SurrealDB SDK| surreal ``` ## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow ```mermaid graph TB claude["🤖 Claude Agent"] github["☁️ GitHub"] dev["👤 Developer"] subgraph cli_boundary ["Brain CLI"] post_commit_hook["post-commit hook
Shell script"] commit_check["commit-check
CLI command
Parse task refs → API call → done"] mcp_server["MCP Server
stdio
update_task_status tool"] end subgraph server_boundary ["Brain Server"] mcp_route["MCP Route
HTTP handler
Receives status updates"] session_lifecycle["Session Lifecycle
Module
abort/reject → ready"] create_session["createAgentSession
Function
Links task to session
NO status change"] webhook_handler["Webhook Handler
HTTP handler
Receives push events"] commit_processor["Commit Processor
Module
Sets done or completed"] task_ref_parser["commit-task-refs
Pure function
Regex task:id extraction"] end claude -->|"MCP"| mcp_server mcp_server -->|"HTTP POST /tasks/status"| mcp_route dev -->|"git commit"| post_commit_hook post_commit_hook -->|"shell exec"| commit_check commit_check -->|"HTTP POST /tasks/status"| mcp_route github -->|"Webhook POST"| webhook_handler webhook_handler -->|"async"| commit_processor commit_processor --> task_ref_parser session_lifecycle -.->|"removed"| create_session style cli_boundary fill:#e8e8e8,stroke:#999 style server_boundary fill:#e8e8e8,stroke:#999 style create_session fill:#f9f,stroke:#c0c,color:#333 ``` ## Component Architecture ### Changes by Module #### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession() - **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update - **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record - **Keep**: Workspace scope validation - **Traces to**: US-1, R1 #### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession() - **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task - **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session - **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup - **Traces to**: US-2, R1 #### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck() - **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`) - **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each - **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error - **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` - **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts` - **Traces to**: US-3, R3 #### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation - **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook - **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check) - **Traces to**: US-4, R4 #### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing - **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias) - **Traces to**: US-3, US-4 #### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit() - **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch: - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done` - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed` - **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch` - **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6 #### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook() - **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`) - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 #### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult - **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability - **Traces to**: US-5, US-6 ### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun. **No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`). ### Idempotent Status Transitions Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers. ### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution) | Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result | |---|---|---|---| | Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done | | Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done | | Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed | | Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed | ## Technology Stack No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack: | Component | Technology | License | Rationale | |---|---|---|---| | CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime | | Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime | | Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store | | Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists | | HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists | ## Integration Patterns ### CLI to Server (commit-check) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status` - **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow) - **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }` - **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0. ### GitHub to Server (webhook) - **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing) - **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler) - **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}` - **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done` ### Agent to Server (in_progress) - **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing) - **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes) - **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes) ## Quality Attribute Strategies ### Reliability - **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor - **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow - **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged ### Maintainability - **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source - **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication - **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists ### Observability - **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA - **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking ### Testability - **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation - **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient` - **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions ## Deployment Architecture No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

content.text:
Good catch. The requirements (R3) specify an LLM fallback when `extractReferencedTaskIds()` returns nothing, reusing the same LLM analysis the GitHub commit processor already has. But the architecture and roadmap don't mention extracting that logic into a shared module.

Let me check what the GitHub processor actually does for LLM analysis.

input.prompt:
In /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/farmerville, find the LLM-based commit analysis logic in the GitHub webhook/commit processor. I need to understand:

1. Where does the GitHub commit processor use an LLM to analyze commit messages and infer which tasks a commit relates to?
2. What function/module does this analysis?
3. What inputs does it take (commit message, task list, etc.)?
4. What model does it use?
5. Is it already a standalone function or tightly coupled to the webhook handler?

Check these files:
- app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts
- app/src/server/webhook/ (all files)
- Any imports from those files that reference LLM/AI/model/generate

Be thorough - search for keywords like "generate", "model", "llm", "ai", "streamText", "generateText", "generateObject" in the webhook directory.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Summary: LLM-Based Commit Analysis Logic

I've located and analyzed the complete commit analysis pipeline in your codebase. Here are my findings:

### 1. **Where the LLM Analysis Happens**

The LLM-based commit analysis is primarily in:
- **`/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/farmerville/app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts`** (lines 1-78)

This module contains the `extractStructuredGraph()` function that uses an LLM to analyze commit messages and infer entities/relationships.

### 2. **What Function/Module Does This Analysis**

**Primary function:** `extractStructuredGraph()`

Located in: `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/farmerville/app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts`

The function flow:
1. Called from commit processor (line 200 in `github-commit-processor.ts`)
2. Uses `generateObject()` from the Vercel AI SDK (`ai` package)
3. Passes the model, schema, and formatted prompt to the LLM
4. Returns structured `ExtractionPromptOutput` with entities, relationships, and tools

### 3. **Inputs the Function Takes**

```typescript
extractStructuredGraph({
  extractionModel: any;           // The LLM instance
  conversationHistory: MessageContextRow[];  // Prior messages (empty for commits)
  currentMessage?: MessageContextRow;        // Optional message metadata
  graphContext: ExtractionGraphContextRow[]; // Prior extracted entities
  sourceText: string;                         // Commit message (enhanced)
  onboarding: boolean;                        // false for commits
  heading?: string;
  workspaceName?: string;         // Workspace context for scoping
  projectNames?: string[];        // Existing projects for hierarchy
})
```

**For commits specifically** (from `processCommit()` at line 200):
- `sourceText` = commit message enhanced with task references and workspace context
- `conversationHistory` = empty array (no conversation context)
- `graphContext` = prior extracted entities from the workspace (up to 60 items)
- `workspaceName` and `projectNames` = used to scope entity classification (project vs feature)

### 4. **Which Model It Uses**

**Default model:** `openai/gpt-4.1-mini` (via OpenRouter)

Configuration flow:
- Loaded from env var `EXTRACTION_MODEL` (defaults to `openai/gpt-4.1-mini` in `.env`)
- Instantiated in `dependencies.ts` via OpenRouter with response healing plugin
- Passed as `extractionModel` to all webhook/extraction functions
- Wrapped with devtools middleware if enabled

The model is instantiated as:
```typescript
const extractionModel = wrap(openrouter(config.extractionModelId, {
  plugins: [{ id: "response-healing" }],
  ...(config.openRouterReasoning ? { extraBody: { reasoning: config.openRouterReasoning } } : {}),
}));
```

### 5. **Is It Standalone or Tightly Coupled?**

**Highly standalone** - excellent architecture:

- **`extractStructuredGraph()`** is a pure, reusable function that:
  - Takes only its inputs as parameters
  - Has no side effects (doesn't write to DB)
  - Can be called from any context (commits, chat messages, documents, etc.)
  - Is NOT tightly coupled to webhook handler

- **Coupling is one-way:**
  - Webhook handler (`github-commit-processor.ts`) calls `extractStructuredGraph()`
  - Both use shared types from `/app/src/server/extraction/types.ts`
  - Both use shared schema validation from `/app/src/server/extraction/schema.ts`
  - Output is then persisted separately via `persistExtractionOutput()`

- **Three-function pattern:**
  1. **`extractStructuredGraph()`** - LLM inference only
  2. **`persistExtractionOutput()`** - Persistence & post-validation
  3. **`classifyDecisionLinks()`** - Decision confidence threshold logic

### How the Commit→Task Linking Works

**Two mechanisms:**

**Mechanism 1: Explicit references (regex-based, not LLM)**
- Function: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` in `/app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`
- Searches for `task:ID` or `tasks: ID1, ID2` patterns in commit message
- Links them directly to the commit with `RELATE task->implemented_by->git_commit`

**Mechanism 2: LLM-inferred decisions (confidence-based)**
- LLM analyzes commit message and extracts decisions/entities with confidence scores
- Function: `classifyDecisionLinks()` in `/app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` (lines 93-107)
- Decision logic:
  - If `confidence >= autoLinkThreshold` (0.85 by default) → auto-link the decision to commit
  - If `confidence < 0.85` → create an observation asking user to confirm
- Auto-linked decisions: `RELATE decision->implemented_by->git_commit`

### Extraction Output Schema

The LLM must return this structure (from `schema.ts`):
```typescript
{
  entities: [{
    tempId: string;
    kind: "project" | "feature" | "task" | "decision" | "question";
    text: string;
    confidence: number;  // 0-1, used for auto-link threshold
    evidence: string;    // verbatim from source
    category: "engineering" | "research" | "marketing" | "operations" | "design" | "sales" | "none";
    priority: "critical" | "high" | "medium" | "low" | "none";
    assignee_name?: string;
    resolvedFromMessageId?: string;
  }];
  relationships: [{
    kind: string;
    fromTempId: string;
    toTempId: string;
    confidence: number;
    fromText: string;
    toText: string;
  }];
  tools: string[];  // Adopted technologies
}
```

Interesting - the LLM analysis in the GitHub commit processor is actually `extractStructuredGraph()` which extracts entities/decisions from commit messages, not specifically for task matching. The task linking is done via two mechanisms:

1. Regex: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` - explicit `task:id` refs
2. LLM: `extractStructuredGraph()` + `classifyDecisionLinks()` - infers decisions from commit messages and links them

But this is about entity extraction, not about "which task does this commit solve". The R3 requirement says "LLM fallback: if no explicit refs found, uses the same LLM analysis as the GitHub commit processor to infer which task(s) the commit relates to."


So the GitHub processor doesn't actually have a direct "which task does this commit solve" capability - it only does task linking via regex. The "LLM fallback" from R3 would need to be a new feature that analyzes a commit message against open tasks to infer which ones it relates to, which is distinct from the entity extraction that `extractStructuredGraph()` does. Let me check what the user originally meant about the LLM fallback behavior.

Looking back at the code, the extraction model always runs rather than serving as a fallback when regex fails, and it extracts entities and decisions, not task references. So I need to update the architecture to clarify that the commit-check CLI command should call a server endpoint that runs the extraction pipeline to infer task matches when explicit references aren't found. This two-pass approach (regex first, then LLM fallback via the server API) is what's actually missing from the current design, and I should update the docs to reflect this.

content.text:
Interesting finding. The GitHub processor's LLM analysis is actually `extractStructuredGraph()` from the extraction pipeline — it extracts entities/decisions from commit messages and uses `classifyDecisionLinks()` for confidence-based auto-linking. The task ref matching itself is regex-only today via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`.

So for R3's "LLM fallback", the commit-check CLI would need to call the server's extraction pipeline when regex finds no refs. Let me update the architecture and roadmap.

## Component Architecture

### Changes by Module

#### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update
- **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record
- **Keep**: Workspace scope validation

#### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task
- **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session
- **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup
- **Traces to**: US-2, R1

#### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck()
- **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`)
- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
- **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error
- **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`

- **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check)

- **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias)

- **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch:
  - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done`

- **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`)
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture Design: Task Status Ownership

## System Context

Task status lifecycle transitions are currently split between the server (orchestrator) and agents/processors, creating ambiguity about source of truth. This refactoring moves **forward transitions** to the entity doing the work (agent, commit hook, GitHub processor) while keeping **backward transitions** (abort/reject) server-owned.

### Capabilities

| Capability | Owner | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| in_progress | Agent | `brain-start-task` slash command calls `update_task_status` MCP tool |
| done | commit-check CLI (local) / GitHub processor (remote) | Task refs parsed from commit messages |
| completed | GitHub processor | Merge to main/default branch detected |
| ready (abort) | Server | `abortOrchestratorSession()` |
| ready (reject) | Server | `rejectOrchestratorSession()` |

### Setup-Dependent Behavior

| Setup | in_progress | done | completed |
|---|---|---|---|
| Solo/local | Agent | commit-check post-commit hook | N/A or manual |
| Team/remote | Agent | commit-check + GitHub processor | GitHub processor (merge) |

## C4 System Context (L1)

```mermaid
graph TB
    dev[Developer]
    lead[Team Lead]
    claude[Claude Code Agent]
    github[GitHub]
    brain[Brain Platform]

    dev -->|post-commit hook| brain
    dev -->|git push| github
    claude -->|MCP: in_progress| brain
    github -->|Webhook: done/completed| brain
    lead -->|Web UI| brain
```

## C4 Container (L2)

```mermaid
graph TB
    dev[Developer]
    github[GitHub]
    claude[Claude Code Agent]

    subgraph brain [Brain Platform]
        cli[Brain CLI - Bun binary]
        server[Brain Server - Bun HTTP]
        surreal[(SurrealDB v3)]
    end

    claude -->|MCP stdio| cli
    cli -->|HTTP POST| server
    dev -->|post-commit hook| cli
    github -->|Webhook POST| server
    server -->|SurrealDB SDK| surreal
```

## C4 Component (L3) -- Status Transition Flow

```mermaid
graph TB
    claude[Claude Agent]
    github[GitHub]
    dev[Developer]

    subgraph cli_boundary [Brain CLI]
        post_commit_hook[post-commit hook]
        commit_check[commit-check command]
        mcp_server[MCP Server]
    end

    subgraph server_boundary [Brain Server]
        mcp_route[MCP Route]
        session_lifecycle[Session Lifecycle - abort/reject]
        create_session[createAgentSession - NO status change]
        webhook_handler[Webhook Handler]
        commit_processor[Commit Processor - done/completed]
        task_ref_parser[commit-task-refs - pure function]
    end

    claude -->|MCP| mcp_server
    mcp_server -->|HTTP POST /tasks/status| mcp_route
    dev -->|git commit| post_commit_hook
    post_commit_hook -->|shell exec| commit_check
    commit_check -->|HTTP POST /tasks/status| mcp_route
    github -->|Webhook POST| webhook_handler
    webhook_handler -->|async| commit_processor
    commit_processor --> task_ref_parser
    session_lifecycle -.->|removed| create_session
```

## Component Architecture

### Changes by Module

#### 1. `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-queries.ts` -- createAgentSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 546-561 conditional `status: "in_progress"` update
- **Keep**: `source_session` linkage to task record
- **Keep**: Workspace scope validation
- **Traces to**: US-1, R1

#### 2. `app/src/server/orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts` -- acceptOrchestratorSession()
- **Remove**: Lines 557-563 `status: "done"` update on task
- **Keep**: `orchestrator_status: "completed"` on session
- **Keep**: Agent session end, handle registry cleanup
- **Traces to**: US-2, R1

#### 3. `cli/commands/git-hooks.ts` -- NEW: runCommitCheck()
- **Add**: New `brain commit-check` command (replaces/extends existing `check-commit`)
- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
- **Constraint**: Fire-and-forget, exit 0 on any error
- **Reuses**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` from `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts`
- **Reuses**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` from `cli/http-client.ts`
- **Traces to**: US-3, R3

#### 4. `cli/commands/init.ts` -- Post-commit hook installation
- **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook
- **Script**: `#!/bin/sh\nbrain commit-check\n` (fire-and-forget, no error check)
- **Traces to**: US-4, R4

#### 5. `cli/brain.ts` -- CLI routing
- **Add**: `commit-check` case (currently only `check-commit` exists; rename or add alias)
- **Traces to**: US-3, US-4

#### 6. `app/src/server/webhook/github-commit-processor.ts` -- processCommit()
- **Add**: After creating `implemented_by` relations, set task status based on branch:
  - Non-default branch push: set referenced tasks to `done`
  - Default branch (main) push: set referenced tasks to `completed`
- **Input**: Branch info available from `GitHubPushEvent.ref` and `GitHubPushEvent.repository.default_branch`
- **Constraint**: Idempotent -- skip if task already at target status or beyond
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6, R5, R6

#### 7. `app/src/server/webhook/github-webhook-route.ts` -- handleGitHubWebhook()
- **Add**: Pass `event.ref` and `event.repository.default_branch` through to `processGitCommits()` (already available in `event`)
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

#### 8. `app/src/server/webhook/types.ts` -- ProcessWebhookResult
- **Add**: `taskStatusUpdates: Array<{ taskId: string; newStatus: string }>` to result types for observability
- **Traces to**: US-5, US-6

### Shared Module: commit-task-refs.ts

The `extractReferencedTaskIds()` function in `app/src/server/webhook/commit-task-refs.ts` is already a pure function with no server dependencies. It can be imported directly by the CLI commit-check command since both CLI and server run on Bun.

**No duplication needed.** The CLI imports the server module directly at build time (CLI is compiled to a binary via `bun build`).

### Idempotent Status Transitions

Status transitions follow a forward-only ordering: `open < todo < ready < in_progress < done < completed`. A transition request is a no-op if the task is already at or beyond the target status. This is enforced at the API layer (`updateTaskStatus` MCP route) rather than duplicated across callers.

### Transition Ordering (conflict resolution)

| Scenario | commit-check fires | GitHub processor fires | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Local commit, then push | Sets done | Sets done (no-op) | done |
| Push to feature branch | May not run (remote CI) | Sets done | done |
| Merge to main | N/A | Sets completed | completed |
| Commit on main directly | Sets done | Sets completed (upgrades) | completed |

## Technology Stack

No new technology introduced. All changes use existing stack:

| Component | Technology | License | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| CLI | Bun + TypeScript | MIT | Existing CLI runtime |
| Server | Bun HTTP | MIT | Existing server runtime |
| Database | SurrealDB v3 | BSL 1.1 | Existing data store |
| Task ref parsing | Regex (existing) | N/A | `extractReferencedTaskIds()` already exists |
| HTTP client | Fetch API (existing) | N/A | `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists |

## Integration Patterns

### CLI to Server (commit-check)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST to `/api/mcp/{workspaceId}/tasks/status`
- **Auth**: Bearer token from `~/.brain/config.json` (existing OAuth2 flow)
- **Payload**: `{ task_id: string, status: "done" }`
- **Error handling**: Fire-and-forget. Log to stderr, exit 0.

### GitHub to Server (webhook)
- **Protocol**: HTTP POST webhook (existing)
- **Trigger**: Push event (existing handler)
- **Branch detection**: Compare `event.ref` against `refs/heads/${event.repository.default_branch}`
- **Status mapping**: default branch = `completed`, other branches = `done`

### Agent to Server (in_progress)
- **Protocol**: MCP stdio (existing)
- **Tool**: `update_task_status` (existing, no changes)
- **Trigger**: `brain-start-task` slash command (existing, no changes)

## Quality Attribute Strategies

### Reliability
- **Idempotent transitions**: Forward-only status ordering prevents conflicts between commit-check and GitHub processor
- **Fire-and-forget hook**: Post-commit hook never blocks git workflow
- **Backward transitions preserved**: Server-owned abort/reject continue unchanged

### Maintainability
- **Single ownership per transition**: Each status change has exactly one authoritative source
- **Shared pure functions**: `extractReferencedTaskIds()` reused without duplication
- **Existing API reuse**: `BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus()` already exists

### Observability
- **Logging**: Each transition logs source (commit-check, webhook, agent) with task ID and commit SHA
- **Result types**: `ProcessWebhookResult` extended with `taskStatusUpdates` for tracking

### Testability
- **Pure function**: Task ref parsing is already testable in isolation
- **HTTP boundary**: commit-check tests mock `BrainHttpClient`
- **Webhook tests**: Existing smoke test (`tests/smoke/github-webhook.test.ts`) extended for status transitions

## Deployment Architecture

No deployment changes. The CLI binary is rebuilt with `bun build` (existing process). Server deploys unchanged. Post-commit hook is installed via `brain init` (existing mechanism, extended).

lines: -- **Behavior**: Read latest commit SHA, extract task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()`, call `updateTaskStatus(done)` for each
+  1. **Fast path**: Parse task refs via `extractReferencedTaskIds()` (regex, instant)

+  2. **LLM fallback**: If no explicit refs found, call server endpoint that runs the same extraction pipeline as the GitHub commit processor (`extractStructuredGraph()` from `app/src/server/extraction/extract-graph.ts`) to infer task matches from the commit message
+#### 3a. Server endpoint for commit analysis -- NEW or REUSE
+- The CLI cannot run the LLM extraction locally (no model access). It calls the Brain server which already has the extraction model wired up.
+- **Option A**: Reuse the existing webhook commit processing endpoint (POST commits to it as if from a local push)
+- **Option B**: Add a dedicated `POST /api/mcp/:workspaceId/commits/check` endpoint that accepts a commit message, runs `extractStructuredGraph()`, and returns inferred task IDs
+- The GitHub commit processor already calls `extractStructuredGraph()` in `processCommit()` — the extraction logic is standalone and reusable
- **Add**: Install `brain commit-check` as `.git/hooks/post-commit` alongside existing pre-commit hook

"02":
    title: "CLI commit-check with post-commit hook"
    steps:
      "02-01":
        title: "Add brain commit-check CLI command with LLM fallback"
        description: >
          New command with two-pass task detection:
          1. Fast path: parse task refs via extractReferencedTaskIds() (regex)
          2. LLM fallback: if no explicit refs, call server endpoint that runs
             extractStructuredGraph() (same extraction pipeline as GitHub commit
             processor) to infer task matches from the commit message.
          For each matched task, call updateTaskStatus(done). Fire-and-forget on errors.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Commit with task:abc123 sets task abc123 to done (regex fast path)"
          - "Commit with tasks: abc, def sets both to done"
          - "Commit without explicit task refs calls server LLM endpoint to infer tasks"
          - "LLM-inferred tasks are set to done"
          - "Already-done task remains done without error"
          - "API failures log to stderr and exit 0"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Reuse extractReferencedTaskIds() from webhook/commit-task-refs.ts"
          - "Reuse BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus() from cli/http-client.ts"
          - "Call server for LLM analysis (CLI has no model access)"
          - "Server reuses extractStructuredGraph() from extraction/extract-graph.ts"
          - "Add commit-check case to cli/brain.ts routing"
        traces: [US-3, R3]

      "02-02":
        title: "Server endpoint for commit analysis"
        description: >
          Add POST /api/mcp/:workspaceId/commits/check endpoint (or reuse existing
          webhook processing path) that accepts a commit message, runs the extraction
          pipeline (extractStructuredGraph + classifyDecisionLinks), and returns
          inferred task IDs. The extraction model and pipeline are already wired in
          the server via dependencies.ts.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Endpoint accepts commit message and returns inferred task IDs"
          - "Uses same extraction model as GitHub commit processor"
          - "Returns empty list when no tasks can be inferred"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Reuse extractStructuredGraph() from extraction/extract-graph.ts"
          - "Reuse extraction model from server dependencies"
          - "No new model initialization needed"

roadmap:
  name: "Task Status Ownership"
  paradigm: functional
  estimated_production_files: 7
  # step_ratio: 6/7 = 0.86 (well under 2.5 threshold)

phases:
  "01":
    title: "Remove server-owned forward transitions"
    steps:
      "01-01":
        title: "Remove in_progress from createAgentSession"
        description: "Remove status promotion from createAgentSession(); keep source_session linkage and workspace validation"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Creating agent session for a ready/todo task does not change task status"
          - "Task source_session is still linked to new session"
          - "Workspace scope validation still rejects cross-workspace tasks"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify only mcp-queries.ts createAgentSession()"
        traces: [US-1, R1]

      "01-02":
        title: "Remove done from acceptOrchestratorSession"
        description: "Remove task status update from acceptOrchestratorSession(); keep session lifecycle (orchestrator_status, handle cleanup, agent session end)"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Accepting session does not change linked task status"
          - "Session orchestrator_status still becomes completed"
          - "Agent session end and handle cleanup still execute"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify only session-lifecycle.ts acceptOrchestratorSession()"
        traces: [US-2, R1]

  "02":
    title: "CLI commit-check with post-commit hook"
    steps:
      "02-01":
        title: "Add brain commit-check CLI command"
        description: "New command reads latest commit message, extracts task refs via extractReferencedTaskIds(), calls updateTaskStatus(done) for each. Fire-and-forget on errors."
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Commit with task:abc123 sets task abc123 to done"
          - "Commit with tasks: abc, def sets both to done"
          - "Commit without task refs produces no status changes"
          - "Already-done task remains done without error"
          - "API failures log to stderr and exit 0"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Reuse extractReferencedTaskIds() from webhook/commit-task-refs.ts"
          - "Reuse BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus() from cli/http-client.ts"
          - "Add commit-check case to cli/brain.ts routing"
        traces: [US-3, R3]

      "02-02":
        title: "Install post-commit hook via brain init"
        description: "Extend brain init to install .git/hooks/post-commit invoking brain commit-check. Fire-and-forget shell script."
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "brain init creates post-commit hook that invokes brain commit-check"
          - "Git commit in initialized repo triggers commit-check automatically"
          - "Hook script exits 0 regardless of commit-check outcome"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Extend cli/commands/init.ts hook installation section"
          - "Follow existing pre-commit hook pattern"
        traces: [US-4, R4]

  "03":
    title: "GitHub processor status transitions"
    steps:
      "03-01":
        title: "GitHub processor sets done on non-main push"
        description: "After creating implemented_by relations, set referenced tasks to done when push is to a non-default branch"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Push to feature branch with task refs sets tasks to done"
          - "implemented_by relation is still created"
          - "Already-done tasks remain done without error"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify processCommit() in github-commit-processor.ts"
          - "Branch info from GitHubPushEvent.ref vs repository.default_branch"
          - "Idempotent: skip if task already at or beyond target status"
        traces: [US-5, R5]

      "03-02":
        title: "GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main"
        description: "When push targets the default branch, set referenced tasks to completed instead of done"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Merge/push to main with task refs sets tasks to completed"
          - "Already-completed tasks remain completed without error"
          - "Non-main branch pushes still set done (not completed)"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Same processCommit() modification, branching on isDefaultBranch"
          - "Extend ProcessWebhookResult with taskStatusUpdates for observability"
        traces: [US-6, R6]

roadmap:
  name: "Task Status Ownership"
  paradigm: functional
  estimated_production_files: 7
  # step_ratio: 6/7 = 0.86 (well under 2.5 threshold)

phases:
  "01":
    title: "Remove server-owned forward transitions"
    steps:
      "01-01":
        title: "Remove in_progress from createAgentSession"
        description: "Remove status promotion from createAgentSession(); keep source_session linkage and workspace validation"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Creating agent session for a ready/todo task does not change task status"
          - "Task source_session is still linked to new session"
          - "Workspace scope validation still rejects cross-workspace tasks"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify only mcp-queries.ts createAgentSession()"
        traces: [US-1, R1]

      "01-02":
        title: "Remove done from acceptOrchestratorSession"
        description: "Remove task status update from acceptOrchestratorSession(); keep session lifecycle (orchestrator_status, handle cleanup, agent session end)"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Accepting session does not change linked task status"
          - "Session orchestrator_status still becomes completed"
          - "Agent session end and handle cleanup still execute"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify only session-lifecycle.ts acceptOrchestratorSession()"
        traces: [US-2, R1]

  "02":
    title: "CLI commit-check with post-commit hook"
    steps:
      "02-01":
        title: "Add brain commit-check CLI command with LLM fallback"
        description: >
          New command with two-pass task detection:
          1. Fast path: parse task refs via extractReferencedTaskIds() (regex)
          2. LLM fallback: if no explicit refs, call server endpoint that runs
             extractStructuredGraph() (same extraction pipeline as GitHub commit
             processor) to infer task matches from the commit message.
          For each matched task, call updateTaskStatus(done). Fire-and-forget on errors.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Commit with task:abc123 sets task abc123 to done (regex fast path)"
          - "Commit with tasks: abc, def sets both to done"
          - "Commit without explicit task refs calls server LLM endpoint to infer tasks"
          - "LLM-inferred tasks are set to done"
          - "Already-done task remains done without error"
          - "API failures log to stderr and exit 0"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Reuse extractReferencedTaskIds() from webhook/commit-task-refs.ts"
          - "Reuse BrainHttpClient.updateTaskStatus() from cli/http-client.ts"
          - "Call server for LLM analysis (CLI has no model access)"
          - "Server reuses extractStructuredGraph() from extraction/extract-graph.ts"
          - "Add commit-check case to cli/brain.ts routing"
        traces: [US-3, R3]

      "02-02":
        title: "Server endpoint for commit analysis"
        description: >
          Add POST /api/mcp/:workspaceId/commits/check endpoint (or reuse existing
          webhook processing path) that accepts a commit message, runs the extraction
          pipeline (extractStructuredGraph + classifyDecisionLinks), and returns
          inferred task IDs. The extraction model and pipeline are already wired in
          the server via dependencies.ts.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Endpoint accepts commit message and returns inferred task IDs"
          - "Uses same extraction model as GitHub commit processor"
          - "Returns empty list when no tasks can be inferred"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Reuse extractStructuredGraph() from extraction/extract-graph.ts"
          - "Reuse extraction model from server dependencies"
          - "No new model initialization needed"
        traces: [US-3, R3]

      "02-03":
        title: "Install post-commit hook via brain init"
        description: "Extend brain init to install .git/hooks/post-commit invoking brain commit-check. Fire-and-forget shell script."
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "brain init creates post-commit hook that invokes brain commit-check"
          - "Git commit in initialized repo triggers commit-check automatically"
          - "Hook script exits 0 regardless of commit-check outcome"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Extend cli/commands/init.ts hook installation section"
          - "Follow existing pre-commit hook pattern"
        traces: [US-4, R4]

  "03":
    title: "GitHub processor status transitions"
    steps:
      "03-01":
        title: "GitHub processor sets done on non-main push"
        description: "After creating implemented_by relations, set referenced tasks to done when push is to a non-default branch"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Push to feature branch with task refs sets tasks to done"
          - "implemented_by relation is still created"
          - "Already-done tasks remain done without error"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Modify processCommit() in github-commit-processor.ts"
          - "Branch info from GitHubPushEvent.ref vs repository.default_branch"
          - "Idempotent: skip if task already at or beyond target status"
        traces: [US-5, R5]

      "03-02":
        title: "GitHub processor sets completed on merge to main"
        description: "When push targets the default branch, set referenced tasks to completed instead of done"
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Merge/push to main with task refs sets tasks to completed"
          - "Already-completed tasks remain completed without error"
          - "Non-main branch pushes still set done (not completed)"
        architectural_constraints:
          - "Same processCommit() modification, branching on isDefaultBranch"
          - "Extend ProcessWebhookResult with taskStatusUpdates for observability"
        traces: [US-6, R6]

content.text:
Updated both the architecture and roadmap:

- **Architecture**: Module 3 now describes the two-pass flow (regex → LLM fallback via server), plus a new module 3a for the server endpoint that wraps `extractStructuredGraph()`
- **Roadmap**: Phase 02 now has 3 steps — 02-01 (CLI command with LLM fallback), 02-02 (server endpoint for commit analysis), 02-03 (post-commit hook installation)

The key insight: the CLI can't run the LLM locally (no model access), so it calls the server which already has the extraction model wired up via `dependencies.ts`.
